headline: Claude Code Dominates, Cursor Levels Up, Copilot Evolves — The Week AI Coding Got Real

stories.title: Cursor 2.6: Interactive UIs & JetBrains Integration ft. Agent Client Protocol
stories.summary: Cursor drops interactive UI support in agent chats, team plugin sharing, and integrates with JetBrains IDEs via Agent Client Protocol for multi-IDE agentic coding—lowkey the move everyone needed fr.
stories.takeaway: Cursor's not just competing, it's expanding. IDEs finally getting real agent support outside VS Code means the agent wars are broadening fast.

stories.title: Claude Code March Madness: 1M Context, Voice Mode & MCP Elicitation Drop
stories.summary: Anthropic ships voice mode (push-to-talk), 1M token window on Max/Team/Enterprise by default, /loop for persistent sessions, and MCP elicitation for agents to request user input—Claude Code went from zero to hero in 8 months fr.
stories.takeaway: Claude Code is literally the most-used AI coding tool now (Pragmatic Engineer survey of 900+ devs). This month's update cement its lead with UX that actually works.

stories.title: GitHub Copilot for JetBrains: Custom Agents & Plan Mode Go GA
stories.summary: Copilot's agentic features—custom agents, sub-agents, plan agent—hit general availability in JetBrains IDEs with agent hooks in preview & MCP auto-approve support, making IDE-native agents a real thing.
stories.takeaway: JetBrains users finally getting proper agent support. This is quietly huge for the 50% of devs not on VS Code.

stories.title: Pragmatic Engineer Survey: 95% of Devs Use AI Weekly, Claude Code Topples Copilot
stories.summary: Survey of 906 engineers shows Claude Code hit #1 in 8 months, 55% use agents regularly (way up from single digits), and Cursor grew 35% since last year—this ain't hype anymore, it's the baseline.
stories.takeaway: AI coding adoption is mainstream now. The real story: agents replacing autocomplete. Staff+ engineers are the agent believers (63.5% usage).

watchNext.title: Simon Willison on Agentic Engineering: The Divide AI Exposed
watchNext.why: Mar 12 post breaking down how AI-assisted coding creates a fork in the road—craft lovers vs. make-it-go people. Honestly fr, if you're building with agents, this reframes what you're actually doing.

watchNext.title: 🎙️ Latent Space: Replit Agent & Cursor Agents with Samantha Whitmore
watchNext.why: Recent pod ep diving into how agents are moving from simple task execution to multi-step reasoning & continuous learning. The guest from Cursor's team breaks down what's actually hard about production agents.
